ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JANE FENG AND ZHOU ZHEN IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT SHOWS THAT THE FRAUD CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE THREE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF CCP 338(D). THE FRAUD CLAIM REFERENCES STATEMENTS BY PLAINTIFFS' FORMER ATTORNEY ARTHUR LIU MADE IN DEMURRER PAPERS THAT WERE FILED IN APRIL 2013, MORE THAN THREE YEARS BEFORE THIS CASE WAS FILED. MOREOVER, THE FRAUD CLAIM COULD HAVE BEEN INCLUDED IN THE PRIOR LAWSUITS BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS AGAINST MR. YANG AND ARE THEREFORE BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE OF RES JUDICATA. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jane Feng and Zhen Zhou (415.876.6703), each appears In Propria Persona as plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Georgett Beck of the law offices of MillerHauser Law Group LLP (415.485.2200) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Certified Mandarin Interpreter, Daniel Hsueh interprets English/Mandarin for the plaintiffs.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT, as to the sole cause of action for fraud in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Jane Feng and Zhou Zhen is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The face of the complaint shows that the fraud claim is barred by the three-year statute of limitations of CCP 338(d). The fraud claim references statements by plaintiffs' former attorney Arthur Liu made in demurrer papers that were filed in April 2013, more than three years before this case was filed. Moreover, the fraud claim could have been included in the prior lawsuits brought by plaintiffs against Mr. Yang and are therefore barred by the doctrine of res judicata. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
